title: athyrium × yakuinsulare
intro: Athyrium × yakuinsulare, a sterile cultivar of the Athyrium genus, is a unique plant species resulting from the cross-breeding of Athyrium yokosukeense and Athyrium filix-femina. This plant, also known as the Yaku Island Lady Fern, is used in traditional medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Athyrium × yakuinsulare include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the fronds, which can be made into teas, infusions, or added to salves and ointments.
